Hello there,

You may not know me, but I’m Representative Elissa Slotkin. I’m a freshman Member of Congress from Michigan -- I flipped a Republican seat in November 2018 and I am proudly representing Michigan’s 8th district. I’m a former CIA officer with 3 tours in Iraq -- and I’m writing to formally introduce myself.

**In November 2018, we proved that it’s time for a new generation of leaders who think bigger, work harder, and never forget that they are public servants first.**

This comes naturally to me, because prior to ever thinking of running for office, I served our country for 14 years. From my service in Iraq, to the White House, to the Pentagon, my entire career has been about protecting the interests and opportunities that belong to us as Americans. And throughout, I believe it has been my straightforward, commonsense approach that has helped me get things done.

I learned those values from my family, and from my upbringing here in Michigan. And that’s what helps me stay laser-focused on practical solutions while I’m representing the folks of Michigan’s 8th district.

So why am I reaching out? Because I am hoping you’ll join the team that’s following and supporting candidates who are defending the House in 2020. I represent a district that went for President Trump by 7 points in 2016, and Michigan is going to be ground zero in the 2020 election.

**If you’re interested, there are a couple ways to stay connected with us as we move forward:**
* You can follow us on social media: Please [**click here to ‘like’ us on Facebook**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8127491/78090988/-279869633?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MDk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZTY3ZWEzZi00OGEyLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KbZa9wFY_9vXQQVbVkjs_pokEX4O99YacxqPTXs6_Ro=) and [**click here to follow us on Twitter.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8127492/78090989/89139515?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MDk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZTY3ZWEzZi00OGEyLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KbZa9wFY_9vXQQVbVkjs_pokEX4O99YacxqPTXs6_Ro=)

* You can let me know what issues matter the most to you! Feel free to reply to this email. Unlike other form emails, we actually do really want to hear from you and will really read every response.

* [**You can make a donation to my re-election in whatever amount you can spare, whether that’s $3, $5, or $20.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8127493/78090990/1742345387?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MDk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZTY3ZWEzZi00OGEyLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KbZa9wFY_9vXQQVbVkjs_pokEX4O99YacxqPTXs6_Ro=&refcode=em_welcome&recurring=auto&amount=20&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) Your support would help us in a big way to continue to get our message out there.

Here’s what I can promise you as I run again: I will always be plain spoken about the issues that matter most to our district and to Michigan, including expanding access to affordable healthcare, lowering prescription drug prices, and improving our crumbling infrastructure. **And I will never send you a form email meant to bully or scare you into supporting me.**

THANK YOU for being part of the team,

Elissa

subject: Voter fraud
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MIKE SIEGEL FOR CONGRESS](https://click
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8131070/78121007/683764388?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzY0MGVhMS01NWEyLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mNCBQC6cjXgNOaShP0iODCQbJnkhQRqU5QwClle6g6s=)
---
Peter, No one should be denied the right to vote because of a clerical error. Yesterday, news outlets across the country reported that former Kansas Secretary of State Kris Kobach, who infamously fought to suppress voting rights by using minor errors to purge voter rolls, was the victim of an election-related misspelling himself. The one-time head of Donald Trump’s so-called “voter fraud commission” announced his run for Senate on Monday. But the paperwork his campaign filed with the Federal Election Commission incorrectly spelled his first name as “Chris.” It’s easy to laugh at this, and we certainly won’t blame you if that was your immediate reaction. But making jokes about it obscures the issue, and it’s a big one: No one should ever be denied the right to vote because of a clerical error. Misspellings and typos are part of everyday life, and even the most fastidious among us makes a mistake from time to time. Voting is a constitutional right, and it is appalling that something as common, innocent, and inconsequential as misspelling a name like “Kris” can be cynically used as a reason to disenfranchise voters.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8131071/78121008/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzY0MGVhMS01NWEyLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mNCBQC6cjXgNOaShP0iODCQbJnkhQRqU5QwClle6g6s=&refcode=em070819) Across the country, voters have reported horror stories of not being allowed to vote, or having their vote thrown out. [I encourage anyone who wants to learn more to read this report on voter suppression in 2018.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8131072/78121009/-407838784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzY0MGVhMS01NWEyLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mNCBQC6cjXgNOaShP0iODCQbJnkhQRqU5QwClle6g6s=)

One of the most notorious examples was, of course, in the state of Georgia: “53,000 voter registrants—70 percent of whom were black—were placed in ‘pending’ status by the secretary of state because of minor misspellings or missing hyphens on their registration forms. A federal judge intervened to stop this practice on November 2, 2018—four days before the election—citing the ‘differential treatment inflicted on a group of individuals who are predominantly minorities.’ However, those with pending registration statuses were still forced to prove eligibility, including U.S. citizenship, before voting on Election Day, which can be difficult for Americans lacking access to birth certificates, passports, or nationalization documents.” My campaign has always fought aggressively against voter suppression. We know that democracy does not work unless everyone has the right to vote, and everyone’s vote counts equally. My campaign didn’t stand for voter suppression at Prairie View A&M; we didn’t stand for it when David Whitely tried to purge thousands of voters at the state level; [and I won’t stand for any illegitimate infringement on voting rights at the federal level when I’m elected to Congress.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8131073/78121010/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzY0MGVhMS01NWEyLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mNCBQC6cjXgNOaShP0iODCQbJnkhQRqU5QwClle6g6s=&refcode=em070819) In the end, Kris Kobach was allowed to correct his misspelling. This unthinking, minor error won’t cost him the right to run for Senate, even if it was embarrassing. It certainly shouldn’t keep anyone from exercising their constitutional right to vote. | [Chip in and help support voting rights! ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8131074/78121011/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzY0MGVhMS01NWEyLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mNCBQC6cjXgNOaShP0iODCQbJnkhQRqU5QwClle6g6s=&refcode=em070819)

For the people,
Mike Siegel

clean_body: = Peter, No one should be denied the right to vote because of a clerical error. Yesterday, news outlets across the country reported that former Kansas Secretary of State Kris Kobach, who infamously fought to suppress voting rights by using minor errors to purge voter rolls, was the victim of an election-related misspelling himself. The one-time head of Donald Trump’s so-called “voter fraud commission” announced his run for Senate on Monday. But the paperwork his campaign filed with the Federal Election Commission incorrectly spelled his first name as “Chris.” It’s easy to laugh at this, and we certainly won’t blame you if that was your immediate reaction. But making jokes about it obscures the issue, and it’s a big one: No one should ever be denied the right to vote because of a clerical error. Misspellings and typos are part of everyday life, and even the most fastidious among us makes a mistake from time to time. Voting is a constitutional right, and it is appalling that something as common, innocent, and inconsequential as misspelling a name like “Kris” can be cynically used as a reason to disenfranchise voters. Across the country, voters have reported horror stories of not being allowed to vote, or having their vote thrown out. = One of the most notorious examples was, of course, in the state of Georgia: “53,000 voter registrants—70 percent of whom were black—were placed in ‘pending’ status by the secretary of state because of minor misspellings or missing hyphens on their registration forms. A federal judge intervened to stop this practice on November 2, 2018—four days before the election—citing the ‘differential treatment inflicted on a group of individuals who are predominantly minorities.’ However, those with pending registration statuses were still forced to prove eligibility, including U.S. citizenship, before voting on Election Day, which can be difficult for Americans lacking access to birth certificates, passports, or nationalization documents.” My campaign has always fought aggressively against voter suppression. We know that democracy does not work unless everyone has the right to vote, and everyone’s vote counts equally. My campaign didn’t stand for voter suppression at Prairie View A&M; we didn’t stand for it when David Whitely tried to purge thousands of voters at the state level; In the end, Kris Kobach was allowed to correct his misspelling. This unthinking, minor error won’t cost him the right to run for Senate, even if it was embarrassing. It certainly shouldn’t keep anyone from exercising their constitutional right to vote. | For the people, Mike Siegel G M T Y | Detect Friend,

_**President Trump is right.**_

It's totally ridiculous that our Nation's government cannot ask a basic question of citizenship in our very EXPENSIVE, very important Census.

The American People deserve to know who is in this country, Friend, and there's only one way to find out:
**We need to ask every person _' ARE YOU AN AMERICAN CITIZEN, YES OR NO?'_**
The President wants to know if you're on his side. All you have to do is answer one simple question:

**Do you believe the 2020 United States Census should ask if people are American citizens? **

In another era, this would be COMMON SENSE... but the _**Left**_ is trying to block President Trump's efforts to ask people if they are citizens of this great Nation.

We can't Keep America Great for all Americans if we don't know who's in this country.

**[Please take our Official 2020 Census Survey NOW to let the President know your thoughts.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=c650c7a5a21488b80e3d435ddf2fdfc80128cb1a788ee9a3a0c85c65c67081e14c9061f1bfcfc2ad80d1056af00cad1e72681c3d7467b460)**

subject: DEMO MEMO: Health Care Is On The Line
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP02/1/67825/images/demo%20memo.jpg]

DEMO MEMO:

This afternoon oral arguments in the Trump-Morrisey fueled Republican attempt to overturn our health care law will begin in a federal appeals court in New Orleans.

Protections for as many as 133 million Americans with pre-existing conditions & that includes 800,000 West Virginians with pre-existing conditions could be gone. The uninsured rate would rise nationally by 65% & prescription drug prices would rise for seniors. If the Medicaid expansion is dismantled, West Virginians who battle opioid addiction could lose treatment options & families could lose their healthcare coverage altogether.

Below are some of the health care protections that are on the line if Patrick Morrisey & Donald Trump are successful.

GONE: Protections for as many as 133 million Americans with pre-existing conditions. The uninsured rate will increase by 65 percent.

GONE: Medicaid expansion, which covers 17 million Americans.

GONE: More than 5 million seniors each year will have to pay more for prescription drugs because the Medicare ‘donut hole’ will be reopened.

GONE: About 2 million adult children will no longer be able to stay on their parents’ insurance.

GONE: Insurance companies will be able to charge women 50 percent more than men.

GONE: Financial assistance that helps 9 million Americans purchase health care in the marketplace.

GONE: Key support for rural hospitals. As Americans lose coverage, already struggling hospitals will be hit even harder as their costs increase.

GONE: Ban on insurance companies having lifetime caps on coverage.

GONE: Requirements that insurance companies cover prescription drugs and maternity care.

Trump and Morrisey campaigned on health care promises to West Virginians that they are breaking.

Trump promised he would put a stop to rising health care costs and even reduce them -- but his lawsuit would raise out-of-pocket costs for premiums and prescription drugs.

Trump promised to support pre-existing condition protections and said that costs for people with pre-existing conditions would be “much lower” under his leadership -- but his lawsuit would end these vital protections.

Trump promised to protect Medicaid -- but his lawsuit would end access to Medicaid for millions of Americans.

---

name: Team Beto
subject: What the media’s not showing you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
#
You can really feel it out there. Momentum for Beto continues to grow. The energy on the ground this weekend was palpable. The Independence Day Parade in Independence, Iowa drew working parents, kids, veterans, climate activists, and people from all walks of life. Just look:
Later in the day, Beto visited Williams, Iowa, a community of 332 – and 100 people showed up. He made time to talk to everyone he possibly could – not one person left out.
He spoke to educators over the weekend too, for the annual National Education Association conference in Houston.
Then, on a holiday Sunday, in a state this campaign had never been to, 1,000 people turned out to our rally in Nashville. "It felt like a University of Tennessee football game in here," (as one local writer put it).
And just yesterday, Beto held a roundtable on immigration, listening to perspectives, ideas, and a diversity of experiences from Dreamers, refugees, asylum seekers, and immigrants from the world over.
This campaign is about going everywhere, showing up for everyone, and focusing on people every single day. And no matter what a few people on Twitter say, we are seeing excitement for Beto in towns and cities everywhere we go.
Our ground-game is strong. We’re not counting anyone out. And Beto’s doing real work that no other candidate is doing. But there’s a danger we won’t have the resources to keep him on the road if enough people don’t pitch in now.
[Chip in $3 or more now to help Beto keep doing what he does best – talking to people, face-to-face, about the new country we can build. ](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/18081?refcode=ema20190709-41437&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=41437%2E444211%2ES_7-C1)

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Border Wall will Help People on Both Sides -- Trump is Right
body:
"After my trip last week to the southern border, my conviction is stronger than ever that President Trump is right: we have to build the wall." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1746975ad0&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Border Wall will Help People on Both Sides -- Trump is Right**

“After my trip last week to the southern border, my conviction is stronger than ever that President Trump is right: we have to build the wall. We have to turn off the magnets that attract . . . encourage and allow migrants to enter our country under false pretenses,” Rep. Roger W. Marshall (R-KS) writes in Fox News.

“Last week I joined six other physicians in Congress and found today’s crisis to be so much worse. Despite Herculean efforts by our Border Patrol officers, we are unable to keep up with the deluge of migrants. Our systems are simply overwhelmed, and there appears to be no break in the near future.”

The bottom line: “While the national press portrays the Border Patrol as the ‘bad guy,’ we should recognize that Border Patrol agents are making the best of a very difficult situation — a crisis created by a badly broken immigration system.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9c5c1964a2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Americans by a wide margin agree with President Trump that the upcoming 2020 census should ask a citizenship question,” Paul Bedard reports for the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0bc6633a3a&e=3ee4985eea). An overwhelming 53 percent of respondents agreed with the President, according to the latest Economist/YouGov poll. Less than a third disagreed.

**Just in** : [55% of Hispanics support U.S. citizenship question on Census, too](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=064fdc6231&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “USMCA improves virtually every component of the old NAFTA, and California’s agriculture industry stands to gain significantly,” Agriculture Secretary Sonny Perdue writes in the [_San Francisco Chronicle_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dd218ac5d2&e=3ee4985eea). “When President Trump mentioned the possibility of withdrawing from NAFTA, the universal hue and cry from agriculture was ‘do no harm.’ Not only has he done that, he negotiated a better agreement on almost every front.”
---
|
---
| President Trump recently signed an executive order to make healthcare prices more transparent by requiring “hospitals to publicly post standard prices for medical care in an ‘easy-to-understand, consumer-friendly’ format that will ‘allow patients to compare prices across hospitals,’” healthcare expert Sally Pipes writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=de66d81bfd&e=3ee4985eea). “Patients' wallets will benefit from more competition. The president's efforts to boost price transparency can help provide just that.”
---
|
---
| Yesterday, Vice President Mike Pence criticized a Democratic member of Congress “for cheapening the Holocaust by comparing the Nazi death camps to U.S. Customs and Border Patrol detention facilities for illegal immigrants,” Dave Boyer reports for [_The Washington Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4dad496532&e=3ee4985eea). “To compare the humane work of dedicated men and women of Customs and Border Protection with the horrors of the Holocaust is an outrage,” the Vice President said.

“The Nazis took lives. American law enforcement saves lives every day.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3572463555&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fd038bd588&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=6eb75f9671)
---

---

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: [Video] What I told the nation about Trump's cruel policies:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2dc4a/6cd27893/4ef3492c/158090ac/261751409/VEsH/ "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")

| | On Sunday, [I was on NBC's _Meet the Press_](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2dc4a/6cd27893/4ef3492c/158090ad/261751409/VEsE/ "http://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1262414063918244") to talk about Donald Trump's cruel immigration policies. We need to make one thing abundantly clear: **whenever someone from Trump's administration says the reports about mistreatment of children and families, about family separation,_any_ of it is untrue or unsubstantiated -- that's just _false_.** I've seen it with my own eyes, but you don't have to take my word for it. The Department of Homeland Security's own Inspector General found that children weren't given hot meals until inspectors showed up. They reported that "At one facility, some single adults were held in standing-room-only conditions for a week." There is a rot at the core of our country, a great evil being carried out in our names. **As I told Chuck Todd, if the American people could see it for themselves, every single one of us would be horrified:** [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2dc4a/6cd27893/4ef3492c/158090aa/261751409/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1262414063918244") The Trump administration's treatment of children, of refugees, of families seeking legal asylum is a generational evil, a cruelty that we may never live down. Right now, they're building the largest child prison in American history. Here's how we start to make it right: continue to shine a light on what's happening, support the victims in every way we can, make sure people are aware of what's happening, and then work to vote out each and every politician who shares responsibility for this crisis. [**Join me on Facebook today. Watch the video of my appearance on _Meet the Press_ and let's talk about the next steps to end Donald Trump's cruel immigration policies.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2dc4a/6cd27893/4ef3492c/158090aa/261751409/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1262414063918244") **_Jeff_** Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | [$5](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2dc4a/6cd27893/4ef3492c/158090ab/261751409/VEsD/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZiMjVsZlgwPSI6IjUifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=2019-07-09-mtp&refcode2=fb-express-buttons") | [$15](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2dc4a/6cd27893/4ef3492c/158090a8/261751409/VEsA/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkSGR2ZlgwPSI6IjE1In0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=2019-07-09-mtp&refcode2=fb-express-buttons") | [$25](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2dc4a/6cd27893/4ef3492c/158090a9/261751409/VEsB/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkR2h5WldWOWZRPT0iOiIyNSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=2019-07-09-mtp&refcode2=fb-express-buttons") | [$50](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2dc4a/6cd27893/4ef3492c/158090a6/261751409/VEsO/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZabTkxY24xOSI6IjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=2019-07-09-mtp&refcode2=fb-express-buttons")

name: Sri Preston Kulkarni
subject: Hi – we must protect our healthcare
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
Healthcare costs are exploding, yet this Administration will stop at nothing to repeal the Affordable Care Act. Today, the 5th Circuit Court of Appeals is hearing oral arguments in the Trump Administration and 18 Republican-led states lawsuit challenging the constitutionality of the Affordable Care Act. If successful, their lawsuit would increase the cost of healthcare, eliminate protections for people with pre-existing conditions, and raise prescription drug costs.

Incredibly, Pete Olson has stood with these efforts every step of the way - endangering the healthcare of millions of Americans. Today, medical bills are the number one cause of bankruptcy in America. My father's medical treatment for cancer nearly bankrupted my family, and I've heard so many stories on the campaign trail just like mine.

It's time to fix what's not working in Washington, not play politics with the livelihood of millions of Americans. [**I 'm ready to take on the fight for our healthcare. Will you stand with me?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/1ZDXVk92PS5M4ijuSG77sg-hCG65_-x1jfBtTNFvuaI=/Iwix_qWJjV4uAEl9QXLbcXsOcrV2ZW4ODyvkVi16MP8=)

I'm running to protect every American's access to healthcare because no family should ever have to go through what mine did. Insurance companies should not be allowed to charge people with pre-existing conditions extreme amounts for basic healthcare. Prescription drug companies shouldn't be allowed to charge whatever they want for medications that literally mean the difference between life and death.

We need leaders who tell folks what they stand for, not just what they're against. I'm standing for access to healthcare, for lowering prescription drug costs, and making sure that the millions of Americans with pre-existing conditions get the care they need and that they deserve. **[Stand with me in standing up for our healthcare.](https://act.myngp.com/el/1ZDXVk92PS5M4ijuSG77sg-hCG65_-x1jfBtTNFvuaI=/Iwix_qWJjV4uAEl9QXLbcXsOcrV2ZW4ODyvkVi16MP8=)**

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/1ZDXVk92PS5M4ijuSG77sg-hCG65_-x1jfBtTNFvuaI=/Iwix_qWJjV4uAEl9QXLbcXsOcrV2ZW4ODyvkVi16MP8=)

Standing strong for our healthcare,

Sri Preston Kulkarni

* * *

body:

|

[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa250/18d6a294/4ba24f0c/26d67a74/4237905903/VEsH/ "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | It's hard to believe that the year is already almost halfway over, so I want to check back in with you. [**_Please tell me what issues are most important to you and your family._**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa250/18d6a294/4ba24f0c/26d67a75/4237905903/VEsE/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190709") While traveling our state, I've gotten to meet so many Tennesseans and have had important conversations. I've heard about the effects of rural hospital closures and the opioid epidemic. I've heard from voters who are concerned about the trade war, and attacks on reproductive and LGBTQ rights. I've heard concerns about the crisis at the border and immigration. And more than anything else, I've heard people say they are just plain sick of the divisiveness of politics in Washington. We need leadership that will look past the divide and look for solutions. I want to be that leader for Tennessee. But in order to fight for you and your family, I need to hear directly from you: [**_What issues matter most to you? Take our survey now >>_**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa250/18d6a294/4ba24f0c/26d67a75/4237905903/VEsF/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190709") I look forward to reading your answers. Thanks, James
---

name: Meg
subject: Only 4 weeks left until the Primary!
body:
|
---
I'll get right to it -- the primary is exactly 4 weeks away today.
Hi friend,

I'll get right to it -- the primary is exactly 4 weeks away today. That's right, **less than a month** until the first voters go to the ballot box in this election!

Your support thus far has been incredible. You've empowered Team Hood to work hard every day to spread Jim's vision for a better Mississippi all across the state.

name: Anna Marta Visky [Our Revolution]
subject: My healthcare horror story is far from unique
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
#
The health insurance and pharmaceutical industries are dead set on preventing every American from getting the healthcare we need and deserve. They spend the profits they make -- from providing us with inadequate care -- on donations to politicians of both parties.

That's why Our Revolution is fighting back with our Medicare for All "Ambulance Tour." We're traveling across the country to pressure politicians -- Republicans and Democrats -- who side with greedy corporations over the well-being of families.
[SUPPORT OUR REVOLUTION](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=1+-+SUPPORT+OUR+REVOLUTION&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")

* * *
_**Below is the personal story of Anna-Marta Visky, an Our Revolution leader who recently hosted a powerful Medicare for All Ambulance Tour event in New Jersey:**_

Becoming a mother opened my eyes to the cruelty of our healthcare non-system. Shortly after my child was born, I was diagnosed with postpartum depression. But I wasn't able to focus on getting better. The co-pays for my therapy put serious financial stress on my young family.

Then, because of my family history of breast cancer, my doctor told me to get an MRI. My insurance would only cover part of the cost -- and that was AFTER I paid a $3,000 deductible. And yet, this same for-profit insurance company can afford to pay its CEO tens of millions of dollars each year.

The outrageous truth is that my healthcare struggle is far from unique. Everyone knows someone with a healthcare horror story. The damage being done on a daily basis is not just sad. It's completely avoidable.

That's why, as chair of Our Revolution in Monmouth County, New Jersey, I'm organizing to hold representatives like Andy Kim and Frank Pallone - chair of the powerful House Committee on Energy and Commerce - accountable to passing Medicare for All.
[](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")
Telling personal stories and doing direct action events are crucial to holding politicians accountable -- and we are seeing the results. After Our Revolution activists took action in deep-red Texas and Kentucky, members of Congress in those states became supporters of Medicare for All.

[That's why I'm reaching out to ask you to help Our Revolution keep shaming Republicans AND Democrats to support healthcare as a right. Can you chip in $27, or whatever your family can afford, to help us push Medicare for All into existence?](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=3+-+Thats+why+Im+reaching+out+to+ask+you+to+&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")
[DONATE $10 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=4+-+DONATE+10+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")

[DONATE $20.20 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=20.20&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=5+-+DONATE+2020+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")

[DONATE $27 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=6+-+DONATE+27+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")

[DONATE $50 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=50&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=7+-+DONATE+50+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")

[DONATE $100 NOW](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=100&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=8+-+DONATE+100+NOW&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")
After Our Revolution activists took action, Representative John Yarmuth, a Democrat from Kentucky, signed on to Medicare for All. [He said: "It's not a matter of if we are going to get single payer, it's a matter of when."](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=9+-+He+said+Its+not+a+matter+of+if+we+are+go&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")

I couldn't agree more. But we also must recognize that the health insurance and drug industry lobbies will do everything they can to stop us. In order to win, we must stand together and demand Medicare for All.

Donate here to directly fund our on-the-ground activism for Medicare for All: [https://go.ourrevolution.com/medicare-for-all](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-medicare-for-all?recurring=auto&amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=10+-+httpsgoourrevolutioncommedicareforall&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190709_1_1")

Anna-Marta

Because Elizabeth doesn't cater to big donors, she can spend more time talking with people across the country about the problems they face -- and working on policy plans to solve them. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852eea3/45c30650/174b61215/669c4ba1/1297933398/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")

When we put together our fundraising report for the second quarter, two stats stood out:

subject: We must continue to find hope
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight
body:
Our democracy is resilient, but it is also fragile. As recent elections have proven and state legislative sessions have magnified, the forces that activists and movement leaders battled fifty years ag |

| | | Friend - Our democracy is resilient, but it is also fragile. As recent elections have proven and state legislative sessions have magnified, the forces that activists and movement leaders battled fifty years ago continue to stifle democracy. As Americans, we cannot resign ourselves to disenfranchisement and dismay. We must find hope in the energy of the many who continue to vote and support access to economic opportunity, health care, and high-quality public education in record numbers. There are some who would argue that more people of color turning out to vote disproves the existence of voter suppression. Yet, in the aftermath of _Shelby County v. Holder_ , we have seen an energized and strategic effort to suppress the votes of communities that have long been excluded from the body politic. These efforts began when underrepresented voters started to affect elections in real and tangible ways, and they will continue without our direct intervention. [**Will you help us deliver free and fair elections for all by making a donation to Fair Fight tonight?**](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa6dc/34a064cc/1916ef0d/703e2df/1727649629/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?refcode=IE070919SYA") Elections are about people, and the people have the right to be heard in every election, every year. With sincere gratitude,
Stacey Abrams

subject: See photos from First Lady Melania Trump’s visit with families affected by opioids
body:
First Lady Melania Trump visited Cabell-Huntington Health Department in West Virginia yesterday to... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=29f06be8b9&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 9, 2019
---
| | |
# **See photos from First Lady Melania Trump’s visit with families affected by opioids **

**First Lady Melania Trump** visited Cabell-Huntington Health Department in West Virginia yesterday to promote that community’s efforts to combat the opioid crisis.

**“We have an opportunity** to move the needle to help families and children who have been impacted by opioid abuse,” the First Lady said. “Meeting with federal, state, and local leaders in Huntington, West Virginia, today proved the power of partnerships and community efforts.”

[In photos: Women of Huntington share their stories with the First Lady](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6c2a0834ab&e=3ee4985eea)

**After a roundtable** with these leaders, Mrs. Trump met with a small group of women who have been affected closely by the crisis. Among the participants were Huntington’s Fire Chief, leadership from the Lily’s Place treatment facility, a representative from a first responder wellness program, a recovery coach, and a recovering mother along with her 5-month-old child.

**Each one** shared stories about their own experiences with America’s opioid crisis, as well as the positive impact the community has had on these families.

**The First Lady** has been an advocate for programs that help change the lives of families who have been hurt by the opioid epidemic. As communities across America have been devastated, the lives of countless children are put in harm’s way. Visits like yesterday’s are a crucial piece of Mrs. Trump’s signature “Be Best” initiative, which focuses on the most pressing issues facing children today.

**“Through BE BEST,** I continue to learn about the many issues and challenges surrounding opioid addiction,” Mrs. Trump said. Using this platform, “we can continue to promote programs and highlight resources available to all those who are seeking help with this national epidemic.”

**Before** **departing West Virginia** , the First Lady took a moment to visit Ritter Park, where [453 American flags](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d9467fc801&e=3ee4985eea) dot a grass field. Each flag represents a child in the foster care system in the Huntington, West Virginia, area—many as a result of the opioid epidemic.

[Learn more about First Lady Melania Trump’s “Be Best” initiative](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=de94b38125&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump welcomes Amir of Qatar**

**This week,** President Donald J. Trump has played host to a special guest: His Highness Sheikh Tamim Bin Hamad Al-Thani, Amir of the State of Qatar.

**“President Trump,** friends: It’s a great honor to join you again in Washington, D.C. Thank you for your continued hospitality,” Qatar’s leader said last night at a dinner hosted by Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin. “My visit this week will reaffirm the ideals we share.”

**🎬****Watch:** [President Trump meets with the Amir of Qatar in the Oval Office](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e55cf66f5e&e=3ee4985eea)

**The U.S.–Qatar alliance** is more important than ever, both in the fight against global terrorism and as part of President Trump’s efforts to restore fair, reciprocal trade. Today, that relationship got even stronger, with the Amir announcing a series of large airway purchases—including five Boeing 777 freighters.

[Read the joint statement from President Trump and the Amir of Qatar.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=606e5b21b9&e=3ee4985eea)

name: Team Beto
subject: Nationwide vigils to protest inhumane conditions for migrants
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
#
On Friday, July 12th, thousands of people will gather for nationwide Lights for Liberty vigils to demand an end to inhumane conditions that migrants and asylum seekers are facing in detention centers.
More than 170 organizations from across the social justice spectrum are hosting or co-sponsoring the Lights for Liberty vigils, and more than 650 local events are happening across the country. Civil rights groups, labor organizers, religious leaders, teachers’ associations, and people from all walks of life are joining.
This is a critical moment in history that demands we stand up, that we make a loud, unified demand._Please don’t miss it._
[If you are interested in participating in a local Lights for Liberty vigil on July 12th, please sign up here.](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/lights-for-liberty/?source=em20190709&t=1&akid=41518%2E444211%2ENl1xn_) You’ll receive info to find a local event near you – or to host your own.
**[Sign up](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/lights-for-liberty/?source=em20190709&t=2&akid=41518%2E444211%2ENl1xn_)**
At the detention centers, teen mothers have been held outside in “dog pounds.” Sick and elderly people have been confined in “icebox” rooms for weeks. Babies as young as four months old have been ripped from their parents’ arms. Kids have died. These vigils will send a powerful message that we will not accept these conditions in our country.
Key Lights for Liberty events are being planned across the country as well as at the sites of detention centers – in El Paso, Texas; Homestead, FL; San Diego, CA; Foley Square, NYC. In Washington DC, people are demanding Congress end the detention centers and impeach Trump.
Beto will be joining the vigil in Peterborough, NH. Many people from our team will be at the event in El Paso. We hope you’ll join, wherever you are:
[Please sign up to be part of a July 12th Lights for Liberty vigil here.](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/lights-for-liberty/?source=em20190709&t=3&akid=41518%2E444211%2ENl1xn_)
Thank you for being part of this,
Team Beto

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=41518%2E444211%2ENl1xn_). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.
